contentProfile.topic: Silent letter spelling patterns and high-frequency word recognition
contentProfile.primarySkill: Orthographic pattern awareness for silent consonants and automatic recognition of high-frequency words
sourceEvidence: Teacher headings distinguish spelling-pattern work from recognition work
Benchmark Advance Unit 9 Week 3 curriculum scope

capturedContent.title: Benchmark Advance Spelling Unit 9 Week 3
capturedContent.rawText:
Benchmark Advance Spelling
Unit 9 Week 3

Silent Letters
sign
know
write
thumb
comb
gnat
knock
knife
wrong
climb

High-Frequency Words
among
building
circle
decided
finally
heavy
include
nothing
special
wheel

wordGroups.label: Silent Letters
evidence: Teacher heading 'Silent Letters' explicitly names a spelling pattern target.
Words contain silent consonants (gn-, kn-, wr-, -mb) that require pattern knowledge for spelling production.
Benchmark Advance curriculum treats silent-letter patterns as spelling-construction skills, not just recognition.

wordGroups.label: High-Frequency Words
evidence: Teacher heading 'High-Frequency Words' signals recognition and fluency goals, not spelling production.
These words are common sight words intended for reading fluency and automatic recognition.
Benchmark Advance high-frequency lists prioritize reading fluency over spelling mastery at grade 2.

reviewRecommendations.reason: The worksheet has multiple target groups with different purposes; parent review should confirm Sunny's routing before the lesson plan is approved.
